reasoning: The probability of the US invading Iran in 2025 to establish territorial control is extremely low. Neither the current nor potential future administrations have shown appetite for major ground wars in the Middle East. Any military action against Iran would almost certainly be limited to airstrikes or naval engagements. A ground invasion would require massive preparation that is not underway, and even island seizures for territorial control are highly unlikely and historically unprecedented in recent US-Iran skirmishes.
